entry.title: Asymmetric Symmetry
entry.summary: We all love symmetry. Balance. Perfection. Order. But that doesn't mean…
entry.content:
We all love symmetry.

Balance.

Perfection.

Order.

But that doesn't mean we should dislike asymmetry.

In fact, many opposites of truth are not lies, but another truth.

Symmetry is perfect.

Asymmetry is imperfectly perfect.

I love asymmetry.

It's where all the juice is.

I'd like to think life is asymmetric.

20 seconds of insane courage can change your family tree.

That's staggering asymmetry.

We all love the curves of a perfect circle.

But we should also love the jagged edges of a market.

Up down. Up down. Up (hopefully).

Let's face it, you wouldn't be in the game if it only went up.

The downs keep you in.

Puts the cork in it.

On one side, we have harmony, balance, perfection.

On the other, we have fun house mirrors.

Which way to look?

Both are equal (even in its inequality).

Both are beautiful.

Both are true.

They are opposites.

And they attract.

To monster outcomes.

May your life be balanced with imbalance.

An imperfect infinity loop.

entry.title: Chill
entry.summary: It's hard to write on weekends. Hard is relative of course.
entry.content:
It's hard to write on weekends.

Hard is relative of course.

I just think today should be a rest day.

An off day.

Relaxed.

So, I won't write today.

Not a single word.

entry.title: How Much?
entry.summary: "There is nothing free in this world, but the grace of…
entry.content:
"There is nothing free in this world, but the grace of God."

Today, I want to talk about cost.

But not in the way you might be thinking.

Because sometimes, the most expensive things in our lives are free.

That is, they cost us the most.

The free things.

Now, some free things are cheap.

Some are priceless.

And some are worse than any loan you could ever sign.

In the business world, they talk about opportunity cost.

It's a fancy way of saying "Is this the best use of X? What do we lose out on by putting it here?"

In this case, X is money.

But X can be anything.

For if you put money here, you can't put it there.

Thus, there is lost.

That's the opportunity cost. You can't put it there, because you put it here.

It's a fine model.

One you should apply to your entire life, not just finances.

At what cost?

Is there a better place to put this (time, attention, energy)?

The answer is almost always a resounding "YES!"

You know what to do here.

And double down on all the times you say "No."

Do more of that.

They say if you have to ask how much it costs, you can't afford it.

But I say, like lots of things, they don't know what the fudge they talking about.

If you can afford it, always ask how much it costs.

You can't afford not to.

entry.title: Phatbergers
entry.summary: I got a confession to make. I want to help more…
entry.content:
I got a confession to make.

I want to help more people.

I really do.

But here's the thing:

I don't like people.

Most of them, anyway.

I'm sure I'm an assburger or whatever they call that blessing.

[Dramatic pause].

You seem alright, though.

I mean, you come here to read this sheet.

You're either crazy or cool.

Or both.

Either way, that's a good start.

I can work with that.

Just not now.

Give it time.

Until then, sorry folks, park's closed.

Sign outside should have told you.

But maybe, someday, I flip it over.

👐🍔

entry.title: Cyber and Space
entry.summary: So I'm dancing with ideas. 🎶 And we held the world,…
entry.content:
So I'm dancing with ideas.

🎶 And we held the world, we were turning it. 🎵

Like I do.

And it occurs to me that there are themes.

Genres.

In my life.

In my interests.

Iterating to Actualization.

Cyber and Space.

Quantum and Sacralization.

You ever wonder why they first called the internet and everything around it cyberspace?

It's this idea that there is so much in there (versus out).

A vastness.

With points of light in the mass of dark.

They say we analogize our brain with the latest technology.

So the brain is a computer, perhaps an ai one, and it is agentic to our soul.

And our universe is a cyberspace of sorts.

And if you really think about it, the only way to reach the real stars out there is to figure it all out inside these little boxes down here.

Cyber and Space.

💻🛸

To boldly go...[FARTS].

𖤂

entry.title: Fist on Chin
entry.summary: Welp. Today is/was a strange day. Sometimes, it be that way.
entry.content:
Welp.

Today is/was a strange day.

Sometimes, it be that way.

It occurs to me, that as little time as I put into this thing, I still need to have some focus.

And my mind is elsewhere.

Accumulating and processing data.

I never like reacting.

I'm pro active.

But this one, it's a thinker.

A best course of (re)actioner.

What's the correct response?

More important than a musings column.

At least for today.

entry.title: I Feel Better When I'm Dancing
entry.summary: When was the last time you cut a rug? I don't…
entry.content:
When was the last time you cut a rug?

I don't mean carpetry.

Is that a word?

I mean dancing.

When's the last time you got down?

Got low?

Got down to business?

When's the last time you danced?

Life is rhythm.

Life is frequency.

Life is motion.

Living rooms make great dance floors.

As do kitchens.

Cars.

Basically everywhere.

Heck, even the clubs. 

But I don't go to them anymore.

So I've been told by the people I love that I have terrible taste in music.

Like for instance, I don't like that iconic band from London named after an insect.

But I do like the radiation boys from Chicago.

What?

You got a problem with that?

You lucky I'm dancing.

🕺

entry.title: Good and Bad
entry.summary: Let's talk about good and bad. Not in an alignment sense,…
entry.content:
Let's talk about good and bad.

Not in an alignment sense, but sort of.

I mean quality. Ability. Competence.

These things are easy to see in professionals like doctors or lawyers.

A good lawyer can save your life.

A good doctor can stop you from being sewed.

Something like that.

But a bad one?

Woo boy.

The thing that I learned is that bad professionals can give great results.

A broken clock, after all.

And the other way.

Good ones can fail.

It's not a foolproof thing, because there are humans involved.

And here's the thing I wrestle with.

Would I rather have a bad lawyer or doctor who produced (through no ability of their own) a great result?

Or, a good one who sheet the proverbial bed?

I know the right way to think is the latter.

You want the latter.

The good on a bad day.

The good always.

But results driven people like me ponder.

Hesitate.

Great results are all that matter.

Right?

Right???

Perhaps I am too particular.

A poorly written cover letter just sours me.

As does a lack of charm. Or too much of it.

These are perhaps not the best way to gauge things, but alas, here we are.

Of course, a good professional delivers good results.

Consistently.

But it's not always so.

So I'll ask you, do you want the result or the intention?

The ends or the means?

You want a blind squirrel or Michelangelo on an off day?

entry.title: Homeland
entry.summary: When you look to the horizon, what do you want to…
entry.content:
When you look to the horizon, what do you want to see?

Are you a mountain person?

⛰

Oceans or lakes?

🌊

Fields?

🏑

Woods?

🌲

Skyscrapers?

🏬

Sand?

🏜

Suburbia?

🏡

What makes you feel like you can exhale and breath in deeply?

What feels like home?

And what feels like just a nice place to visit?

And can I really, and I mean really, make a musings column only asking questions?

No.

entry.title: Sandwich
entry.summary: Alright. It's time to talk about punches. Now, there are a…
entry.content:
Alright. It's time to talk about punches.

Now, there are a couple of ways to go about it.

But today I want to talk about knuckles.

Are you a two knuckle (index, middle) or a three knuckle (middle, ring, pinky) puncher?

I was always taught to hit with the big two, but always landed three knuckle.

Just felt more natural.

You want to make sure your knuckles are aligned with your wrist, so that your fist has the support of the entire forearm upon impact. A bent or misaligned fist is no fun.

And it's a snapping motion. 

Relax, relax, whip, snap, retract.

Like a snake.

Do not clench hard when starting the punch. 

Contract at impact (give or take).

Relax.

Step. Snap. Step back.

Step. Snap snap. Step back.

Step. Snap snap snap. Step back.

Good.

If your knuckles are scratched, it's because you're scraping down on the target.

Pop 'em.

In and out.

Jabs and crosses are straight lines.

Hooks and uppercuts are circles.

Everything starts and ends at your feet.

Center and turn at the waist.

The knuckles are just the follow-through of shoulders.

The inevitable.

Snap.

Now come back.

Relax.

Good.

👊🥪
